app_permissions.description: Permissions granted to the GitHub application.

app_repo_url.description: URL of the repository the GitHub application has access to.

app_settings_url.description: URL of the app installation settings.

badge_regex.description: Regex used to identify badge URL.

file_regex.description: Regex used to identify file name.

parent_tool_name.description:
Name of parent tool that this evidence belongs to.
For example, the parent tool is "ghactions" if a reference to "uses: endorlabs" is found in a GitHub Actions workflow file.

url_regex.description: Regex used to identify webhook URL.

id.description: Internal Endor Labs enum ID for this CIS benchmark requirement.

index.description: CIS benchmark requirement index. For example, "2.1.4".

satisfied.description: True if this CIS benchmark requirement is satisfied.

version.description: CIS benchmark requirement version. For example, "v1.0.0".

ContextContextType.description:
- CONTEXT_TYPE_MAIN: Objects from a scan of the default branch.
All objects in the oss namespace are in the main context.
The context id is always "default".
 - CONTEXT_TYPE_EXTERNAL: Indicates that this object is a copy/temporary value of an object in
another project. Used for same-tenant dependencies. In source code
reference this is equivalent to "vendor" folders. Package versions in
the external context are only scanned for call graphs. No other
operations are performed on them.
 - CONTEXT_TYPE_CI_RUN: Objects from a PR scan. The context id is the PR UUID.
Objects in this context are deleted after 30 days.
 - CONTEXT_TYPE_SBOM: Objects from an SBOM scan. The context id is the SBOM serial number or
some other unique identifier.
 - CONTEXT_TYPE_REF: Objects from a scan of a specific branch. The context id is the branch
reference name.

DependencyMetadataReachabilityType.description:
- REACHABILITY_TYPE_REACHABLE: Call graph analysis determined that the dependency is likely reachable.
 - REACHABILITY_TYPE_UNREACHABLE: Call graph analysis determined that the dependency is likely not
reachable.
 - REACHABILITY_TYPE_UNKNOWN: Not enough information to perform reachability analysis.

all_secrets_exposed.description: Information about the exposure of 'secrets' object.

jobs.description: All jobs defined in the workflow file.

permissions.description: Permissions of the GITHUB_TOKEN defined at the workflow file level.

GitHubWorkflowsWorkflowJob.description: A single job defined in the workflow file.

actions.description: List of all actions used in the job.

cloud_resource_accessed_without_oidc.description: Lists all cloud resources accessed without the workflow identity federation.

imposter_commit.description: Imposter commit SHA detected for the job.

permissions.description: Permissions of the GITHUB_TOKEN defined at the job level.

script_injection_detected.description: Detected script injection in the job's shell script code.

unpinned_commit_sha_actions.description: Unpinned commit SHA action versions used.

unpinned_runner_version.description: Unpinned runner version used to run the job.

untrusted_code_execution.description: Untrusted code execution identified for the job.

GroupResponseGroupData.description: Information about objects matching the given key.

aggregation_count.description:
Number of objects in group.
This field is always set.

aggregation_uuids.description:
List of UUIDs of the objects in the group.
Only populated if show_aggregation_uuids is set.

aggregation_value.description: Value of the aggregation operation as calculated by the query.

unique_counts.description:
Map of counts for the given unique_count_paths fields.
Only populated if unique_count_paths is set.

unique_values.description:
Map of values for the given unique_value_paths fields.
Only populated if unique_value_paths is set.

IngestedObjectIngestedObjectStatus.description:
- INGESTED_OBJECT_STATUS_LIGHT: Example: Use when a user exists because they are attached to a commit,
but are not found on a direct lookup.

action_name.description: Name of the action used to access cloud resources.

evidence_message.description: Contains evidence message for the non-OIDC cloud resource violation.

input_parameters.description: List of input parameters used to access the cloud resource.

invoking_script.description: Script content if the cloud resource is accessed using a script.

location_uri.description: Workflow file path with line number for non-OIDC cloud resource access.

step_name.description: Step name for the non-OIDC cloud resource access.

action_repo_clone_url.description: Constructed clone URL used for the lookup.

location_uri.description: Workflow file path with line number for the imposter action step.

step_name.description: Step name where the imposter action is used.

gh_context_variables.title:
List of GitHub context variable for which the injection was detected.
https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-guides/security-hardening-for-github-actions#understanding-the-risk-of-script-injections

location_uris.description: Full path of each injection variable detected with line number.

script_content.description: Shell script content where the injection was detected.

step_name.description: Step name where script injection was detected.

line_number.description: Line number of the workflow file containing the action.
Line number of the workflow file containing the runner version.

name.description: Name of the targeted runner type.

build_cmd.description: Build cmd used to execute the untrusted code.

build_cmd_script_content.description: Shell script content where the untrusted code is being executed.

build_cmd_step.description: Step name where the untrusted code is being executed.

checkout_cmd.description: Checkout command used to checkout the code.

checkout_script_content.description: Shell script content where the untrusted code is being checked out.

checkout_step.description: Step name where the untrusted code is being checked out.

location_uri.description: Workflow file path with the line number for untrusted code execution script.

line_number.description: Line number of the workflow file containing the action.

schema.description: The schema defining the structure and validation rules for the content.

NVDVulnerabilityCvssMetricV2.description: Based on schema definition provided by NVD here. https://csrc.nist.gov/schema/nvd/api/2.0/external/cvss-v2.0.json.

NVDVulnerabilityCvssMetricV30.description: Based on schema definition provided by NVD here. https://csrc.nist.gov/schema/nvd/api/3.0/external/cvss-v2.0.json.

NVDVulnerabilityCvssMetricV31.description: Based on schema definition provided by NVD here. https://csrc.nist.gov/schema/nvd/api/2.0/external/cvss-v3.1.json.

NVDVulnerabilityCvssMetricV40.description:
CvssMetricV40. Based on schema definition provided by FIRST.ORG for CVSS v4.0.
schema in https://csrc.nist.gov/schema/nvd/api/2.0/external/cvss-v4.0.json.

PullRequestSecurityReviewResultInfoChang.description:
ChangeDescription provides a detailed explanation of specific changes made within a file.
Used to track and document individual modifications for better understanding of the changes.

line.title: Starting line number of the change

line_end.title: Ending line number of the change

PullRequestSecurityReviewResultInfoChang.description:
ChangeKind indicates the type of modification made to a code element (file, function, or snippet)
within the pull request. This helps track whether items are newly added, modified, or removed.

 - CHANGE_KIND_UNSPECIFIED: Default state when the change type is unknown
 - CHANGE_KIND_NEW: Indicates a newly added element
 - CHANGE_KIND_MODIFIED: Indicates an existing element that was modified
 - CHANGE_KIND_REMOVED: Indicates an element that was removed

PullRequestSecurityReviewResultInfoCodeS.description:
CodeSnippet represents a specific portion of code that has security implications.
This could be sensitive operations, security controls, or potential vulnerabilities.

change_kind.title: Whether this snippet is new, modified, or removed

description.title: Description of what the code snippet does

file.title: File path where the snippet is located

impact.title: Security impact or implications of this code

line.title: Starting line number of the snippet

line_end.title: Ending line number of the snippet

snippet.title: The actual code content of the snippet

PullRequestSecurityReviewResultInfoFileC.description:
FileChanges tracks all modifications made to a specific file in the pull request.
Provides a comprehensive view of how a file was changed and its security implications.

added_lines.title: Number of lines added to the file

change_kind.title: Overall state of the file (new, modified, removed)

purpose.title: Overall purpose or role of the file

removed_lines.title: Number of lines removed from the file

PullRequestSecurityReviewResultInfoSecur.description:
SecurityAspectMetric represents a specific security concern or aspect identified during review.
Used to quantify and qualify various security characteristics of the changes.

description.title: Detailed description of the security concern

security_impact.description: The security impact of the code change.

severity_level.title: Severity level of the security aspect

snippets.title: Code snippets related to this security aspect

title.title: Short title describing the security aspect

PullRequestSecurityReviewResultInfoSecur.description:
SecurityRelevantFunction represents a function or method that performs security-sensitive operations.
or requires special security consideration during review.

change_kind.title: Whether this function is new, modified, or removed

security_labels.title: Security classification or sensitivity level of the function

PullRequestSecurityReviewResultInfoSecur.description:
SecurityCategory represents the different categories of security aspects that can be evaluated.

 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_UNSPECIFIED: Default when category is unknown
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_DEPENDENCY: Integration of external code, packages, and frameworks
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_ACCESS_CONTROL: Authentication, authorization mechanisms and session management
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_API_ENDPOINT: API and service entry points
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_DATABASE: Database architecture and security
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_CRYPTOGRAPHIC: Cryptographic systems and implementations
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_PAYMENT_PROCESSING: Payment processing and financial systems
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_MEMORY_PROTECTION: Memory management and protection
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_PII_DATA_HANDLING: Processing of PII and sensitive information
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_INPUT_VALIDATION: Input validation and processing security
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_INFRASTRUCTURE: Infrastructure security
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_CI_CD: CI/CD pipeline security
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_CONFIGURATION: Application and infrastructure configuration
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_NETWORK: Network security and communication protocols
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_AI: AI/LLM integration security
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_IMPROVEMENT: Improvement to the security posture
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_VULNERABILITY: Vulnerability found in the code
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CATEGORY_BUG: Bug found in the code

SecurityReviewConfidenceLevel represents the confidence level in the security assessment.

 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CONFIDENCE_LEVEL_UNSPECIFIED: Default confidence level when confidence is unknown
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CONFIDENCE_LEVEL_LOW: Low confidence level
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CONFIDENCE_LEVEL_MEDIUM: Medium confidence level
 - SECURITY_REVIEW_CONFIDENCE_LEVEL_HIGH: High confidence level

Level represents the severity or importance level of a security aspect or finding
Used to prioritize security concerns and determine the urgency of required actions.

 - SECURITY_SEVERITY_LEVEL_UNSPECIFIED: Default level when severity is unknown
 - SECURITY_SEVERITY_LEVEL_LOW: Minor security concerns with minimal impact
 - SECURITY_SEVERITY_LEVEL_MEDIUM: Moderate security concerns that should be addressed
 - SECURITY_SEVERITY_LEVEL_HIGH: Significant security concerns requiring prompt attention
 - SECURITY_SEVERITY_LEVEL_CRITICAL: Critical security issues that need immediate remediation

SecurityAspectMetricSecurityImpactType.description:
Indicates whether a code change improves security, introduces regression, or is neutral.

 - SECURITY_IMPACT_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Default unspecified value.
 - SECURITY_IMPACT_TYPE_IMPROVEMENT: The change improves security.
 - SECURITY_IMPACT_TYPE_REGRESSION: The change introduces security regression.
 - SECURITY_IMPACT_TYPE_NEUTRAL: The change has neutral security impact.

cpes.description: List of CPEs associated with the affected package.

fixed.description: The version or commit in which this vulnerability was fixed.

introduced.description:
The earliest version or commit in which this vulnerability was
introduced.

SpecCVSSSeverityLevel.description: Common severity level enum used across different CVSS versions.

level.description: Level of the base severity. This is computed based on the score.

score.description:
The Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS score) provides a
numerical (0-10) representation of the severity of an information
security vulnerability.

temporal_level.description: Level of the severity. This is computed based on the score.

temporal_score.description:
The Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS score) provides a
numerical (0-10) representation of the severity of an information
security vulnerability.

temporal_vector.description:
A specially formatted vector indicating the attack surface and severity
of the vulnerability. Format is here:
https://www.first.org/cvss/specification-document.

vector.description:
A specially formatted vector indicating the attack surface and severity
of the vulnerability. Format is here:
https://www.first.org/cvss/specification-document.

base_level.description: Level of the base severity. This is computed based on the base score.

base_score.description:
The Common Vulnerability Scoring System v4.0 base score provides a
numerical (0-10) representation of the severity of an information
security vulnerability.

environmental_level.description: Level of the environmental severity. This is computed based on the environmental score.

environmental_score.description:
The CVSS v4.0 environmental score provides context-specific scoring
based on the deployment environment.

threat_level.description: Level of the threat severity. This is computed based on the threat score.

threat_score.description:
The CVSS v4.0 threat score provides additional context about the
exploitability of the vulnerability.

vector.description:
A specially formatted vector indicating the attack surface and severity
of the vulnerability. Format is here:
https://www.first.org/cvss/v4.0/specification-document.

affected_callpath_uris.description:
Affected function URIs in FastenURI format.
For example,
"/com.atlassian.connect.spring.internal.lifecycle/LifecycleController.installed(%2Fcom.atlassian.connect.spring.internal.lifecycle%2FLifecycleEvent,%2Fcom.atlassian.connect.spring%2FAtlassianHostUser)%2Forg.springframework.http%2FResponseEntity".

affected_filepaths.description:
Class name that this vulnerability affects in JVM notation.
For example,
"com/atlassian/connect/spring/internal/lifecycle/LifecycleController.class".

database_specific.description:
Optional. JSON object holding additional information about the
vulnerability as defined by the database for which the record applies.

ecosystem_specific.description:
Optional. JSON object holding additional information about the
vulnerability as defined by the ecosystem for which the record applies.

fix_commits.title: Links to the commits that fix the vulnerability

maintainer_cvss_level.description:
maintainer_cvss_level is the severity level of the vulnerability as determined
by the maintainer. It will determine the finding level if present.

name.description: The name to give the credit.

artifact_id.description: Indicates the artifact artifact_id that the CVE affects.

endor_uri.description:
Affected function URIs in FastenURI format.
For example,
"/com.atlassian.connect.spring.internal.lifecycle/LifecycleController.installed(%2Fcom.atlassian.connect.spring.internal.lifecycle%2FLifecycleEvent,%2Fcom.atlassian.connect.spring%2FAtlassianHostUser)%2Forg.springframework.http%2FResponseEntity".

fixed_versions.description:
A list of all fixed versions.
Can be more than one if the fix is applied on multiple release branches.

group_id.description: Indicates the artifact group id that the CVE affects.

versions_range.description: Vulnerable version range in Maven notation. For example, "[1.1.0,2.1.3)".

vulnerable_filepath.description:
Class name that this vulnerability affects in JVM notation.
For example,
"com/atlassian/connect/spring/internal/lifecycle/LifecycleController.class".

vulnerable_versions.description: A list of all affected artifact versions.

WorkflowAllSecretsExposed.description: AllSecretsExposed defines the exposure of the 'secrets' object.

evidence.description: Matched keyword from the content of the line.

line_content.description: Content of the line exposing full 'secrets' object.

location_uri.description: Full path of exposed 'secrets' object with the line number.

WorkflowGHTokenPermission.description: GHTokenPermission represents the possible permissions assignable to GITHUB_TOKEN used by the workflow.

endorv1Metric.description: This contains the definition all metrics.

spec.description: The internal specification of a metric.

tenant_meta.description: Metrics are associated with a tenant and tenant information must be provided.

uuid.description: The UUID of the the metric resource.

endorv1Vulnerability.description: Vulnerability models information that can be extracted by analyzing CVEs.

additional_notes.description: Notes by the person that processed the CVE.

component.description:
Information about affected artifacts, versions
and function identifiers.

cve_description.description: A freeform textual summary of the vulnerability.

cve_id.description: The CVE ID, as it appears in the NVD. For example, CVE-2021-36090.

cve_references.title: Web links containing extra information about the CVE

cvss_score.description:
The Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS score) provides a numerical
(0-10) representation of the severity of an information security
vulnerability.

cvss_vector.title:
A specially formatted vector indicating the attack surface and severity of
the vulnerability. Format is here:
https://www.first.org/cvss/specification-document

cwe.title:
The Common Weakness Enumeration field, if any.
See: https://cwe.mitre.org

deepdive.description:
Indicates whether the research team performed full analysis on multiple
artifact_ids involved in CVE.

disputed.description:
Indicates whether the research team considers a CVE should be disputed
based on analysis.

ecosystem.title: A free form name of the ecosystem, e.g. Maven

fix_commit.title: Links to the commit(s) that fix the vulnerability

last_updated.description: The timestamp when the vulnerability was last updated.

malicious.description: Indicates whether this item is classified as malicious or not.

nofix.description:
Indicates whether the fix is not released or not disclosed for a CVE.
In such cases, the endor_uri string might not be available.

package_name.title:
A free form name of the affected package / project, e.g. Apache
commons-compress

schema_version.description: The schema version used for this vulnerability descriptor.

withdrawn.description: Indicates whether the underlying vulnerability has been withdrawn.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

database_specific.description:
Optional. JSON object holding additional information about the
vulnerability as defined by the database for which the record applies.

ecosystem_specific.description:
Optional. JSON object holding additional information about the
vulnerability as defined by the ecosystem for which the record applies.

name.description: The name to give credit to.

fixed.description: The version/commit that this vulnerability was fixed in.

introduced.description:
The earliest version/commit where this vulnerability
was introduced in.

limit.description: The limit to apply to the range.

ecosystem.description:
Required. The ecosystem for this package. 
For the complete list of valid ecosystem names, see
<https://ossf.github.io/osv-schema/#affectedpackage-field>.

name.description:
Required. Name of the package. Should match the name used in the package
ecosystem (e.g. the npm package name). For C/C++ projects integrated in
OSS-Fuzz, this is the name used for the integration.

purl.description: Optional. The package URL for this package.

repo.description:
Required if type is GIT. The publicly accessible URL of the repo that can
be directly passed to clone commands.

type.description: Required. The type of version information.
Required. The type of the reference.
The type of this severity entry.

osvVulnerability.description:
A vulnerability entry.
The protobuf representation is *NOT* stable and only used for implementing
the JSON based API.

affected.description: Required. Affected commit ranges and versions.

aliases.description: Optional. IDs for the same vulnerability in other databases.

database_specific.description:
Optional. JSON object holding additional information about the
vulnerability as defined by the database for which the record applies.

details.description: Required. Any additional human readable details for the vulnerability.

id.description:
The `id` field is a unique identifier for the vulnerability entry. It is a
string of the format `<DB>-<ENTRYID>`, where `DB` names the database and
`ENTRYID` is in the format used by the database. For example:
“OSV-2020-111”, “CVE-2021-3114”, or “GHSA-vp9c-fpxx-744v”.

modified.description: The RFC3339 timestamp indicating when this entry was last modified.

published.description: The RFC3339 timestamp indicating when this entry was published.

references.description:
Optional. URLs to more information/advisories (including the
scheme e.g "https://").

related.description:
Optional. List of IDs of closely related vulnerabilities, such as the same
problem in alternate ecosystems.

summary.description:
Required. One line human readable summary for the vulnerability. It is
recommended to keep this under 120 characters.

withdrawn.description:
Optional. The RFC3339 timestamp indicating when this entry is considered to
be withdrawn.

v1BranchAccess.description: BranchAccess contains normalized branch protection settings.

allow_create.description: Anyone with write access to the repository can create a new branch.

allow_delete.description: Anyone with write access to the repository can delete a branch.

allow_force_push.description: Anyone with write access to the repository can force push to a branch.

allow_fork_sync.description: Users can pull changes from upstream when the branch is locked.

allow_review_bypass.description: Specific users, teams, or apps can bypass pull request requirements.

allow_self_approval.description: Users can approve their own pull requests.

dismiss_stale_approvals.description: Approving reviews are automatically dismissed when someone pushes a new commit.

enforce_admins.description: All configured restrictions are also enforced for administrators.

linear_history.description: Linear commit Git history is enforced, which prevents anyone from pushing merge commits to a branch.

num_approvals_required.description: A minimum number of approvals is required to merge a pull request.

require_code_owner_approval.description: Pull request merges are blocked until code owners have reviewed.

require_comment_resolution.description: All pull request comments must be resolved before it can be merged.

require_status_checks.description: All status checks must pass before merging.

require_update.description: Branch must be up to date before merging.

restrict_dismiss.description: Specific users, teams, and apps can dismiss pull request reviews.

restrict_push.description: Specific users, teams, and apps can push code.

v1CategoryScore.description:
The score for one score category. The is the overall score
for a specific category. It is computed from the score factors that
have been created for the category. There is going to be only a single
instance of this information for the repo per score category.

Example:

Category: SCORE_CATEGORY_BEST_PRACTICES

Score: 5

Description: This score provides an overview of how this repo does when considering security best practices.

The value is a number in the range 1 to 10, with 10 being the best.

centered_score.description:
Centered score for the category. This score is
normalized to be centered on 5 and is a float, so it has a higher resolution.

raw_score.description:
Raw score for the category. This is the score by
adding up the score factors, not centered around 5.

categories.description: List of control function categories (based on static name to category mapping).

evidence.description: Details of where and/or how the tool was discovered.

v1CiCdTools.description: CiCdTools Metric Value contains information about CI/CD tools discovered in the repository version.

v1CisCategory.description:
CisCategory represents a CIS benchmark requirement category per
CIS Software Supply Chain Security - Base v1.0.0
https://workbench.cisecurity.org/benchmarks/7555.

v1CisReqId.description:
CisReqId represents a CIS benchmark requirement per
CIS Software Supply Chain Security - Base v1.0.0
https://workbench.cisecurity.org/benchmarks/7555.

v1CisScorecard.description:
CisScorecard represents the CIS benchmark scorecard for the parent repository.
A scorecard contains a list of benchmarks.

v1CisSubCategory.description:
CisSubCategory represents a CIS benchmark requirement sub-category per
CIS Software Supply Chain Security - Base v1.0.0
https://workbench.cisecurity.org/benchmarks/7555.

v1Context.description: Contexts keep objects from different scans separated.

id.description: The context ID, such as a pull request ID or branch reference.

tags.description:
A list of tags applied to a context. Used primarily for CI and SBOM
contexts.

type.description: Context type, usually defined based on how endorctl is being used.

will_be_deleted_at.description:
Time that all objects in this context will be deleted.
This field is deprecated and will be removed in the future.
Please use the meta.will_be_deleted_at field instead.

v1CountResponse.description: Response to a list count request.

count.description: Number of objects matching the given list parameters.

v1DependencySpec.description: Captures the details of a single dependency.

dependency_name.description: The name of the dependency package.

reachable.description: Set to true if the dependency is used from the code in this package.

unpinned.description: Set to true if the dependency is not pinned.

utilization.description: The fraction of the dependency that is used by this package.

dependency_specs.description:
A list of all the related dependencies
for this package or package version.

direct_dependencies.description: The total number of direct dependencies.

indirect_dependencies.description: The total number of indirect dependencies.

v1Ecosystem.description:
- ECOSYSTEM_GO: GoLang.
 - ECOSYSTEM_MAVEN: Maven.
 - ECOSYSTEM_PYPI: Python.
 - ECOSYSTEM_CARGO: Rust.
 - ECOSYSTEM_NPM: Javascript.
 - ECOSYSTEM_GEM: Ruby.
 - ECOSYSTEM_NUGET: Dotnet.
 - ECOSYSTEM_PACKAGIST: PHP.
 - ECOSYSTEM_SBOM: SBOMs.
 - ECOSYSTEM_RPM: RPM.
 - ECOSYSTEM_DEBIAN: Debian.
 - ECOSYSTEM_GITHUB_ACTION: GitHub Actions.
 - ECOSYSTEM_COCOAPOD: Cocoapods.
 - ECOSYSTEM_APK: APK (alpine et.al).
 - ECOSYSTEM_CONTAINER: Containers.
 - ECOSYSTEM_HUGGING_FACE: Hugging Face.
 - ECOSYSTEM_C: C/C++.
 - ECOSYSTEM_GIT: ecosystem GIT for GIT repository dependencies.
This can be used for package name of the resolved dependencies when a
given repository has dependencies to other GIT repositories. Currently we
use this to represent vulnerabilities for the given GIT repository. ex:
git submodules, C/C++ dependencies.
 - ECOSYSTEM_AI_MODEL: AI models.
 - ECOSYSTEM_SWIFT: Ecosystem Swift consists of native Swift packages, which are defined
using the Package.swift manifest file and managed by the Swift Package
Manager. There is a separate ecosystem for Cocoapod packages called
ECOSYSTEM_COCOAPOD, which is an alternative package manager for Swift
packages.

v1FileInfo.description: The breakdown for various file categories.

v1FileStats.description: File statistics message. Included as the payload of a metric object.

badges.description: The names of the badges in the repository.

best_practices_files.description: The names of files that represent best practices.

cicd_files.description: The names of files that contain CI/CD configuration.

copyrights.description: The list of copyright notices found in source files.

documentation_files.description: The names of files that contain documentation.

file_info.description: The overall file information (file count, line count, byte count).

file_types.description: The breadown for each file type.

has_codeowners.description: Whether the version contain a valid code owners file or not.

has_license.description: Whether there is a licence or not.

has_security_md.description: Whether the version contain a SECURITY.md file or not.

languages.description: The breadown for each language type.

make_files.description: The names of files that are related to Make tools.

sast_files.description: The names of files that are related to SAST tools.

test_files.description: The names of files that contain tests.

v1GitHubWorkflows.description: GitHubWorkflows Metric Value contains information about the workflow file's code posture management.

v1GroupAggregationValueResponse.description:
GroupAggregationValueResponse returns the value of the aggregation if
requested.

v1GroupResponse.description: Response to a list group request.

v1IndexData.description:
IndexData is used to index the resource for search. It's an internal
object.

search_score.description: search_score is the score of the resource for search. Internal use only.

will_be_deleted_at.description: Time that the resource will be deleted.

v1IngestedObject.description:
Raw ingested object from a 3rd party source,
along with any additional metadata we want
to add to the object.

ingestion_time.description: Time the object was last ingested.

status.description: Current status of the ingested object.

structured_content.description: The structured content of the LLM context.

unstructured_content.description: The unstructured content of the LLM context.

v1LicenseData.description:
Contains a summary of license information for a project or package.
This will be included as the payload of a metric object.

all_licenses.description: All the licenses found by Endor Labs.

code_licenses.description: The licenses found in the source code.

copyrights.description: The list of copyright notices found in source files.

package_manager_licenses.description: The licenses from the package manager.

repository_license.description: The license of the repository from the SCM system.

v1LicenseInfo.description: LicenseInfo contains information for a license.

file.description: The name of the file where the license was found.

file_location.description: The line in the file where the license text begins.

mapping_info.description: Additional context about the raw license description.

matched_text.description: The license text that was matched.

name.description:
Raw license description as found, for example in package managers.
It is free form text that may not contain a valid SPDX ID.

type.description: License classification (based on licenseclassifier by Google).

url.description: The URL that points to the license description.

v1LicenseMappingInfo.description:
LicenseMappingInfo contains additional information
that we determine when we attempt to match a license string
to a known SPDX id.

 - LICENSE_MAPPING_INFO_NOT_OSS: This does not look to like an OSS license.
 - LICENSE_MAPPING_INFO_IN_CODE: License information is to be found in the code.
 - LICENSE_MAPPING_INFO_UNKNOWN: Cannot determine anything about the license.
 - LICENSE_MAPPING_INFO_NOT_INCOMPLETE: There is some license information, but is not detailed enough.
 - LICENSE_MAPPING_INFO_PRIVATE: Appears to be a private package.
 - LICENSE_MAPPING_INFO_EXACT: An exact match to a SPDX id.
 - LICENSE_MAPPING_INFO_UNLICENSED: Seems to be explicitly without license.

v1LinterResultInfo.description: A brief summary of linter results.

critical_api_category.description: The critical API category of the result.

file.description: The file where the result was found.

line.description: The line in the file where the result was found.

rule_id.description: The rule that generated the result.

snippet.description: The source code that triggered the result.

v1LinterStats.description: The linter statistics message. Included as the payload of a metric object.

distinct_file_types_with_results.description: The count of distinct file types with results.

distinct_rules_with_results.description: The count of distint rules with results.

file_cnt_by_category.description: The breakdown of distinct file count per category.

file_cnt_by_critical_api_category.description: The breakdown of distinct file count per critical API category.

file_cnt_by_file_type.description: The breakdown of distinct file count per file type.

file_cnt_by_rule.description: The breakdown of distinct file count per rule.

file_cnt_by_severity.description: The breakdown of distinct file count per severity level.

files_with_result_count.description: The number of files with results.

finding_results.description:
A list of the results that are marked with ANALYTICS_FINDING, which results
in raising findings.

results.description: A list of all the linter results.

results_cnt_by_category.description: The breakdown of result count per category.

results_cnt_by_critical_api_category.description: The breakdown of result count per critical API category.

results_cnt_by_file_type.description: The breakdown of result count per file type.

results_cnt_by_rule.description: The breakdown of result count per rule.

results_cnt_by_severity.description: The breakdown of result count per severity level.

results_per_filecnt_ratio_by_file_type.description: The ratio of result count/file count per file type.

results_per_filecnt_ratio_by_severity.description: The ratio of result count/file count per severity level.

results_per_loc_ratio_by_file_type.description: The ratio of result count/LOC per severity per file type.

results_per_loc_ratio_by_severity.description: The ratio of result count/LOC per severity level.

v1ListMetricsResponse.description: Response to the request to retrieve the metrics.

next_page_id.description: The UUID of last message retrieved.

next_page_token.description: Pagination token that can be used to retrieve the next page of results.

v1Meta.description: Common fields for all Endor Labs resources.

annotations.description:
Annotations can be used to attach metadata to a resource message.
Annotation values can be small or large, structured or unstructured,
and may include characters not permitted by labels.
The keys may contain alphanumerics, underscores (_), dots (.) and dashes
(-). The values of an annotation must be 16384 bytes or smaller.

create_time.description:
Time the resource was created.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

created_by.description:
Name and authentication source of the user who created the object,
for example, ewok@endor.ai@google@api-key.

description.description: Resource description. Must be less than 1024 bytes.

kind.description:
Resource kind, for example, HelloResponse.
Auto-generated using the protobuf message proto.MessageName().Name().

name.description: Resource name. Must be 63 characters or less.

parent_kind.description: Parent object resource kind, for example, Project.

references.description: Map of objects referenced in a query API.

tags.description:
List of tags attached to the resource.
Tags can be used to select objects and to find collections of objects that
satisfy certain conditions. A tag must be 255 characters or less.

update_time.description:
Time the resource was last updated.
Note: Updated on all create/patch/delete operations.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

updated_by.description:
Name and authentication source of the last user who updated the object,
for example, vulnerabilityingestor@endor.ai@x509.

upsert_time.description:
Time the resource was last upserted.

Note:
create_time is only set the first time the resource is created.
upsert_time is set every time the resource is upseted.

Format: 2017-01-15T01:30:15.01Z
RFC 3339: https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt.

analytic.description: The name of the analytic used to generate this specific metric.

metric_values.description:
A map of metric values.
These values include scores and score factors.

project_uuid.description: The UUID of the project to which this metric relates.

raw.description: This is a superset of the information included in the specification.

v1MetricValue.description: The value of the metric and the associated category.

v1NVDVulnerability.description:
NVD Vulnerability.
Based on schema definition provided by NVD here. https://csrc.nist.gov/schema/nvd/api/2.0/cve_api_json_2.0.schema.

v1OrganizationAccess.description: OrganizationAccess contains normalized organization access settings.

admins.description: List of all organization administrator usernames.

allow_internal_repo_create.description: Allows members to create internal repositories.

allow_private_repo_create.description: Allows members to create private repositories.

allow_private_repo_fork.description: Allows members to fork private repositories.

allow_public_repo_create.description: Allows members to create public repositories.

default_workflow_permissions.description:
Default GITHUB_TOKEN permissions for workflows.
Can be one of: "read", "write".

mfa_external.description: Multi-factor authentication is required for external contributors.

mfa_internal.description: Multi-factor authentication is required for internal contributors.

repo_edit_permission.description:
Default permission required to execute EDIT commands on repository.
Can be one of: "read", "write", "admin", "none".

repo_get_permission.description:
Default permission required to run GET queries on repository.
Can be one of: "read", "write", "admin", "none".

workflows_can_approve_prs.description: Allows workflows to approve pull request reviews.

v1PlatformSource.description: Type of source control platform a resource was discovered on.

v1PullRequestSecurityReviewResultInfo.description:
PullRequestSecurityReviewResultInfo represents the comprehensive results of an automated security review
performed on a pull request. It includes detailed information about code changes, security metrics,
and relevant code snippets that may have security implications.

file_changes.description:
Comprehensive information about all file changes in the pull request.
The map key is the file path.

llm_context.description: Context information about the LLM (Language Learning Model) used for the security review.

security_metrics.title:
Security review findings and metrics
Detailed security metrics and findings

v1RepositoryAccess.description: RepositoryAccess contains normalized repository access settings.

admins.description: List of all repository administrator usernames.

advanced_security_enabled.description: Advanced security is automatically enabled for new repositories.

allow_auto_merge.description: Repository allows auto-merge on pull requests.

allow_merge_commit.description: Repository allows merging pull requests with a merge commit.

default_branch_protection_status.description: Branch protection status of default branch.

pushed_at.description: Time of the most recent commit to the repository.

require_web_commit_signoff.description: Require developers to sign off on commits made through a web interface.

v1ScoreCard.description: A scorecard contains a total score per ScoreCategory.

v1ScoreCategory.description:
The types of scores that Endor Labs tracks.

 - SCORE_CATEGORY_POPULARITY: Tracks a project's popularity.
 - SCORE_CATEGORY_ACTIVITY: Tracks the amount of activity around a project.
 - SCORE_CATEGORY_BEST_PRACTICES: Tracks how much a project follows various development best practices.
 - SCORE_CATEGORY_SUSPICIOUS_ACTIVITY: Tracks suspicious activity on a project.
 - SCORE_CATEGORY_DEPENDENCIES: Tracks the dependencies of a project.
 - SCORE_CATEGORY_SECURITY: Tracks security aspects of a project.
 - SCORE_CATEGORY_CODE_QUALITY: Tracks the code quality of a project.
 - SCORE_CATEGORY_OPERATIONAL_RISK: Tracks the operationa risk of a project.

v1ScoreFactor.description:
A score factor for an object. Each score factor
describes a piece of information used while computing the overall score.
Each factor is associated with a specific category. Score factors can have a
positive impact on the repository score and then they have a positive score, or can
have a negative impact on the overall score and then they have a positive score.
The description captures additional information for the particular factor.
In general there are many (tens or hundreds) of such score factors split across the ScoreCategory categories.

Postive score factor example:

 Category: SCORE_CATEGORY_BEST_PRACTICES

 Score: 2

 Description: The repository is enforcing signed commits.

Negative score factor example:

Category: SCORE_CATEGORY_VULNERABILITIES

Score: -1

Description: the repository has unresolved issues related to vulnerabilities that have been open for more than 3 months.

description.description: Text description of the factor and its meaning.

evidence.description: The details of what the factor was raised.

id.description: Unique ID to identify the factor.

name.description: An external name for the factor.

score_float.description: The float value of the score for the factor.

v1ScoreFactorList.description: A list of score factors that are directly exported by the related anlaytics.

branch_access.description: Branch-level protection settings indexed by branch name.

platform_source.description: The platform where the source code of the project is located.

v1SecretInfo.description: A brief summary of secret results.

file.description: The file where the result was found.

line.description: The line in the file where the result was found.

rule_id.description: The rule that generated the result.

snippet.description: The source code that was responsible for triggering the result.

v1SecretStats.description: The secret statistics message. This is included as the payload of a metric object.

distinct_file_types_with_secrets.description: The count of distinct file types with secrets.

distinct_rules_with_results.description: The count of distint rules with results.

file_cnt_by_category.description: The breakdown of distinct file count for each category.

file_cnt_by_file_type.description: The breakdown of distinct file count for each file type.

file_cnt_by_rule.description: The breakdown of distinct file count for each rule.

file_cnt_by_severity.description: The breakdown of distinct file count for each severity level.

files_with_secrets_count.description: The number of files with secrets.

results_cnt_by_file_type.description: The breakdown of result count for each file type.

results_cnt_by_rule.description: The breakdown of result count for each rule.

results_cnt_by_severity.description: The breakdown of result count for each severity level.

secrets.description: A list of all the linter results.

v1TenantMeta.description: Tenant related data for the tenant containing the resource.

namespace.description:
Namespaces are a way to organize organizational units into virtual
groupings of resources. Namespaces must be a fully qualified name,
for example, the child namespace of namespace "endor.prod" called "app"
is called "endor.prod.app".

v1TimeSlot.description:
Structure that contains activity information for a given slot of time.
Each slot could  capture activity for one day, month or year, depending on the resolution used.

count.description: The count of activity in the slot.

tag_activity.description:
The breakdown of activity per tag. When we track an activity,
it can be associated with a user specified tag (for example, "Open" for a PR).
So here we get the per-tag breakdown.

timestamp.description:
Timestamp of the slot. The resolution of this timestamp
depends on the time resolution we are tracking, so for
daily resolution will be the 12:00am of the day.

v1TimeTracker.description:
Tge sequence of TimeSlots for each time resolution we track.
We can track multiple resolutions, and some resolutions may be empty.

v1Vuln.description: Vuln represents an Endor Labs vulnerability in the system.

additional_endor_notes.description: Notes from the Endor Labs analysis of the vulnerability.

additional_notes.description: Notes by the person that processed the CVE.

aliases.description: Optional. IDs for the same vulnerability in other databases.

cvss_v4_severity.description: Optional. CVSS v4.0 severity of the vulnerability.

database_specific.description:
Optional. JSON object holding additional information about the
vulnerability as defined by the database for which the record applies.

deepdive.description:
Indicates whether the research team performed full analysis on multiple
artifact_ids involved in CVE.

disputed.description:
Indicates whether the research team considers a CVE should be disputed
based on analysis.

malicious.description: Indicates whether this item is classified as malicious or not.

modified.description: The RFC3339 timestamp indicating when this entry was last modified.

published.description: The RFC3339 timestamp indicating when this entry was published.

related.description:
Optional. List of IDs of closely related vulnerabilities, such as the
same problem in alternate ecosystems.

withdrawn.description:
Optional. The RFC3339 timestamp indicating when this entry is considered
to be withdrawn.

v1VulnerabilityStats.description: VulnerabilityFactors message includes all vulnerability related data.

vulnerabilities.description: A list of all the vulnerabilities found affecting this package or package version.
